trainingItems.title: Variable
trainingItems.description: Learn how to declare, assign, and use variables to store data in C#.

codeChallenge.title: Sum of Two Numbers
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read two integers, each on its own line, and print their sum.

Example:
Input: 3 (first line) and 5 (second line)
Output: 8

trainingItems.title: If Else
trainingItems.description: Conditional logic with if-else — decision-making in code.

codeChallenge.title: Number Sign Checker
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read one integer and print:
- "Positive" if it is greater than zero
- "Negative" if it is less than zero
- "Zero" if it equals zero

trainingItems.title: Switch
trainingItems.description: Using switch-case for multi-branch conditionals efficiently.

codeChallenge.title: Day of the Week
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read an integer (1–7) and print the corresponding day name.
1 = Monday, 2 = Tuesday, 3 = Wednesday, 4 = Thursday,
5 = Friday, 6 = Saturday, 7 = Sunday.
For any other number, print "Invalid".

trainingItems.title: Loop
trainingItems.description: Repeating tasks with for, while, and do-while loops.

codeChallenge.title: Count to N
codeChallenge.prompt: Read one integer N and print all numbers from 1 to N, each on its own line.

trainingItems.title: Function
trainingItems.description: Writing reusable blocks of code with methods and return values.

codeChallenge.title: Calculate Factorial
codeChallenge.prompt:
Complete the Factorial method so that it returns N! (N factorial).
Remember: 0! = 1, and N! = N × (N-1) × ... × 1.

trainingItems.title: List
trainingItems.description: Storing and working with collections of data using List<T>.

codeChallenge.title: Reverse a List
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read N on the first line, then N integers each on their own line.
Print the numbers in reverse order, each on its own line.

trainingItems.title: Filtering List
trainingItems.description: Using LINQ and loops to filter collections based on conditions.

codeChallenge.title: Filter Even Numbers
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read N on the first line, then N integers each on their own line.
Print only the even numbers, each on its own line.
If there are no even numbers, print nothing.

trainingItems.title: String Manipulation
trainingItems.description: Working with text — substring, replace, split, and formatting.

codeChallenge.title: Reverse and Uppercase
codeChallenge.prompt:
Read one line of text.
Print it reversed and converted to uppercase.

trainingItems.title: Run & Debug
trainingItems.description: F5 to run, setting breakpoints, and stepping through code to find issues.

trainingItems.title: Solution Explorer
trainingItems.description: Understanding the file and project structure of a .NET solution.

trainingItems.title: NuGet
trainingItems.description: Installing and managing third-party packages with NuGet Package Manager.

trainingItems.title: References
trainingItems.description: Adding references to external assemblies and understanding project dependencies.

trainingItems.title: Github
trainingItems.description: Using Git and GitHub inside Visual Studio for version control and collaboration.

trainingItems.title: Visual Studio Quiz
trainingItems.description: Test your Visual Studio knowledge before moving on.

questions.question: What keyboard shortcut starts debugging in Visual Studio?
What shortcut adds or removes a breakpoint on the current line?
Which panel shows the files and project structure of your solution?
What does 'Step Over' (F10) do while debugging?
options: Executes the current line without entering method calls

questions.question: What is NuGet used for in Visual Studio?
Which keyboard shortcut builds the entire solution?
What does F11 do while debugging?
Where can you see the current values of variables while debugging?
What is a 'Reference' in a Visual Studio project?
options: A link to an external library or assembly

questions.question: What does 'Manage NuGet Packages' allow you to do?

trainingItems.title: Type of Application
trainingItems.description: Understanding what a Windows Forms desktop application is and when to use it.

trainingItems.title: How it Works
trainingItems.description: The event-driven model — how forms, controls, and events interact at runtime.

trainingItems.title: How to Design a UI
trainingItems.description: Using the Visual Studio designer to drag, drop, and configure controls.

trainingItems.title: Windows Form Quiz
trainingItems.description: Test your Windows Forms knowledge before moving on.

questions.question: What type of application is a Windows Forms app?
Which base class does every Windows Form inherit from?
What is an 'event' in Windows Forms?
options: An action triggered by user interaction such as clicking or typing

questions.question: How do you create a button click handler using the Visual Studio designer?
options: Right-click the button and choose Properties
Double-click the button in the designer

questions.question: What does Application.Run() do in a Windows Forms app?
options: Starts the message loop and shows the main form

questions.question: Which control displays text that the user cannot edit?
What property sets the text shown in a Form's title bar?
What file contains the auto-generated layout code created by the designer?

trainingItems.title: How to Connect with Tekla
trainingItems.description: Setting up your project and establishing a connection to a running Tekla Structures model.

trainingItems.title: Model Object and Picker Class
trainingItems.description: Working with model objects and using the Picker class to select objects interactively.

trainingItems.title: Read and Write UDA
trainingItems.description: Reading and writing User Defined Attributes on Tekla model objects.

trainingItems.title: Tekla Open API Quiz
trainingItems.description: Test your Tekla Open API knowledge before moving on.

questions.question: What is the main namespace for Tekla Structures API model objects?
How do you check if your app is connected to Tekla Structures?
What class represents a steel beam in the Tekla Structures API?
What method saves a new object into the Tekla model?
What is a UDA (User Defined Attribute) in Tekla?
options: A custom data field attached to model objects

questions.question: Which class lets the user interactively pick objects from the Tekla model?
What method saves all pending changes back to the Tekla model?
To read a string UDA from a model object, which method do you use?

trainingItems.title: Learn More About Github
trainingItems.description: Branching, pull requests, merge conflicts, and team workflow with Git and GitHub.

trainingItems.title: Error Handling
trainingItems.description: Using try-catch-finally, custom exceptions, and writing resilient code.

trainingItems.title: Debugging Code
trainingItems.description: Advanced debugging techniques — call stack, watch expressions, conditional breakpoints.

trainingItems.title: 3D Geometry Basics
trainingItems.description: Points, vectors, coordinate systems, and basic transformations used in Tekla API.
